cpt.description: Emergency department visit, high medical decision complexity

carc.description: Non-covered service вЂ” payer determined service not medically necessary.
carc.appealNotes: Frequently overturned when admitting diagnosis clinically supports the service. Cite ACEP or specialty guidelines. ER visits for infectious, cardiac, or neurologic diagnoses are almost always upheld. Request peer-to-peer review between your physician and the reviewing physician.

patientFacingSummary: Your insurer denied $2127 that may be wrongful вЂ” you have 2 appealable denials with a strong chance of reversal. We've drafted a ready-to-send appeal letter to help you recover this amount.
appealLetter:
**INTERNAL APPEAL вЂ” LEVEL 1**

Date: June 28, 2026

BlueCross BlueShield of Illinois
Appeals and Grievances Department
[Address]

**RE: Appeal of Denied Claim**
**Claim ID:** 48291
**Member ID:** BCB-2204819334
**Date of Service:** June 14, 2026
**Procedure Code:** 99285

Dear Appeals Review Committee:

I am writing to formally appeal the denial of the above-referenced claim. The explanation of benefits indicated denial code CO-50, stating the emergency department visit was "not medically necessary."

I respectfully disagree with this determination. I presented to Memorial Hospital Emergency Department and was diagnosed with pneumonia (ICD-10: J18.9). Pneumonia is a serious lower respiratory infection that can rapidly progress to respiratory failure, sepsis, and death without prompt evaluation and treatment. The American College of Emergency Physicians recognizes acute respiratory illness with suspected pneumonia as an appropriate emergency department presentation requiring high medical decision-making complexity, consistent with CPT 99285. The emergent nature of my symptoms necessitated immediate diagnostic workup and clinical stabilization, which constitutes the accepted standard of care.

**Pursuant to my rights under ERISA В§503 and applicable Illinois insurance regulations, I formally request the following information regarding the physician who reviewed and denied this claim:** the reviewer's full name and professional title; medical license number and state(s) of licensure; board certifications and specialty area(s); and confirmation whether they personally reviewed my complete medical record. As this denial cited medical necessityвЂ”a clinical determinationвЂ”it must be rendered by a qualified peer physician. If the reviewing physician's specialty is not relevant to emergency or pulmonary medicine, this constitutes grounds for appeal.

I respectfully request that BlueCross BlueShield of Illinois reconsider this determination and provide a written response within 30 days.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Jane Q. Smith
Member ID: BCB-2204819334
